highlights.title: Shared bolus safety checks for Shortcuts & APNS
changes: Boluses started via Shortcuts are now checked for max dose, max IOB, and recent-bolus limits and can be blocked.
Remote (APNS) bolus commands keep their previous behavior but now run the same shared safety checks.
If current IOB is unavailable, bolus commands are rejected with a logged message instead of causing an error.
Shortcut bolus rejection messages are localized and now explain why the bolus was blocked.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1108: Shared BolusSafetyValidator for APNS and Shortcut boluses
Shared BolusSafetyValidator for APNS and Shortcut boluses

highlights.title: Settings search jumps to and highlights matches
changes: Search results now open the correct settings screen and jump to the exact matching control.
The matching control is briefly highlighted so you can spot it quickly.
If a match is inside a conditional or hidden field, search routes you to the visible parent control.
This scroll-and-highlight behavior is enabled across multiple settings sub-screens.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1165: Settings Search with Scroll-to and Highlight for the Matched Result

highlights.title: Scroll-to and highlight for settings search
changes: Tapping a settings search result now scrolls directly to the matching setting.
The matched setting briefly flashes to highlight it after navigation.
This initial update enables the behavior for Dynamic Settings; more screens will be opted in later.
Search terms that differ from on-screen labels can map to the exact setting so the app scrolls to the correct item.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1051: Add scroll-to and highlight for settings search results

highlights.title: Settings search: scroll-to and highlight matches
changes: Search results now scroll to the matching setting and briefly highlight it.
Common or abbreviated search terms map to the exact on-screen labels so results land on the right row.
Many settings screens were updated so search can navigate to and highlight their sections.
Search index labels were corrected for Bolus Calculator and User Interface entries to match on-screen labels.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1096: Add scroll targets and fix search index labels for settings search

highlights.title: Apple Health flag now reflects per-type permissions
changes: Trio will report Apple Health as enabled as soon as any write permission is granted.
Telemetry now includes per-type write permission statuses for glucose, insulin, carbs, fat, and protein.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1164: Fix Apple Health flag by checking for sample type permission

items.title: Settings search scrolls and highlights matches
changes: Selecting a setting from search now scrolls to and highlights the matching option in affected settings screens.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1094: Add settingsHighlightScroll modifier to settings sub-screens

items.title: Code formatting cleanup
changes: Only code whitespace and formatting were changed.
There is no change to app behavior or user experience.

items.title: Fix build configuration
changes: A project build setting was corrected to resolve a build error.
This change does not affect app features or user-visible behavior.

includedPullRequests.title: Add scroll-to and highlight for settings search results
includedPullRequests.body:
## Summary

Adds scroll-to and highlight behavior when tapping a settings search result. Previously, search navigated to the correct screen but left the user to find the setting themselves. Now it scrolls directly to the setting and briefly highlights it.

This is the first PR of what will I'm sure be several. This contains the core logic and enables it for Algorithm > Dynamic Settings. Remaining screens will follow in subsequent PRs (many will add a single `.settingsHighlightScroll()` line per view).

## How it works

Two view modifiers drive the feature:

- **`.settingsHighlightScroll()`** Added once per destination view. Wraps the view in a `ScrollViewReader` and watches for a highlighted setting, then scrolls to it.
- **`.settingsSearchTarget(label:)`** Added to each setting row. Sets `.id(label)` so `ScrollViewReader` can find it, and adds a highlight animation that flashes when the label matches.

`SettingInputSection` (the shared component used by most settings) applies `.settingsSearchTarget(label:)` automatically using its existing `label` parameter. Most screens will only need a single `.settingsHighlightScroll()` line to opt in. Custom sections (like the Dynamic ISF picker) will need a `.settingsSearchTarget(label:)` added manually.

The search result list now uses `NavigationLink(value: SearchResultTarget(...))` which carries both the destination screen and the scroll target label. On navigation, the label is passed to `SettingsSearchHighlight` (an `@Observable` object in the environment), which triggers the scroll and highlight.

`scrollTargetLabels` on `SettingItem` handles cases where the search term differs from the UI label (e.g. searching "Max IOB" scrolls to "Maximum Insulin on Board (IOB)"). This mapping is data-only and has no effect until a screen opts-in with `.settingsHighlightScroll()`.

## Demo

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e8743199-cb06-431f-afb3-48f92045a9de

## Scroll demo on smaller screen.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/51a5d7b0-7dc7-4a84-b5fd-45dbc6183215

includedPullRequests.title: Add settingsHighlightScroll modifier to settings sub-screens
includedPullRequests.body:
- Adds .settingsHighlightScroll() view modifier to 16 settings sub-screens that were missing it
- This enables the scroll-to and highlight behavior when navigating to a setting from search results

includedPullRequests.title: Add settingsHighlightScroll modifier to settings sub-screens
includedPullRequests.body:
- Adds .settingsHighlightScroll() view modifier to 16 settings sub-screens that were missing it
- This enables the scroll-to and highlight behavior when navigating to a setting from search results

includedPullRequests.title: Settings Search with Scroll-to and Highlight for the Matched Result
includedPullRequests.body:
## Summary
Settings search used to surface the right sub-screen but drop you at the top, leaving users to scan for the setting that
actually matched. This PR makes search results jump directly to the matched control and highlight it briefly.

##  Changes
  - New `SettingsSearchHighlight.swift` view modifier (`.settingsHighlightScroll(…)`) that pairs a settings row with the
  search target, scrolls to it on tap-through, and animates a transient highlight.
  - Modifier applied to 16 settings sub-screens so every searchable surface participates.
  - `scrollTargetLabels` mappings on each `SettingItem` for cases where the search hit isn't itself a visible row, e.g.,
  "Carbs Required Threshold" → "Show Carbs Required Badge" (the threshold field only renders when the badge is enabled),
  "High Glucose Target" → "Enable SMB With High Glucose", Bolus Calculator conditional fields, etc. Keeps the user-typed
  term as the entry point while routing to the visible parent.
  - Introduced `SettingsSearchTests.swift` unit coverage for the mapping resolution

Addresses https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/issues/1095
Authored by @gordolio.

## Summary
Settings search used to surface the right sub-screen but drop you at the top, leaving users to scan for the setting that
actually matched. This PR makes search results jump directly to the matched control and highlight it briefly.

##  Changes
  - New `SettingsSearchHighlight.swift` view modifier (`.settingsHighlightScroll(…)`) that pairs a settings row with the
  search target, scrolls to it on tap-through, and animates a transient highlight.
  - Modifier applied to 16 settings sub-screens so every searchable surface participates.
  - `scrollTargetLabels` mappings on each `SettingItem` for cases where the search hit isn't itself a visible row, e.g.,
  "Carbs Required Threshold" → "Show Carbs Required Badge" (the threshold field only renders when the badge is enabled),
  "High Glucose Target" → "Enable SMB With High Glucose", Bolus Calculator conditional fields, etc. Keeps the user-typed
  term as the entry point while routing to the visible parent.
  - Introduced `SettingsSearchTests.swift` unit coverage for the mapping resolution

Addresses https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/issues/1095
Authored by @gordolio.

includedPullRequests.title: Add scroll targets and fix search index labels for settings search
includedPullRequests.body:
Part 3 of #1095 

- Replace .listRowBackground(Color.chart) with .settingsSearchTarget(label:) on 7 User Interface sections so they become scroll targets for search
- Add .settingsHighlightScroll() to User Interface screen
- Add scrollTargetLabels mappings for Units/Limits, SMB, Target Behavior, Additionals, and User interface
- Fix incorrect searchContents in Bolus Calculator and User Interface

## About `scrollTargetLabels`
`scrollTargetLabels` maps the search term to the actual UI label:
```swift
scrollTargetLabels: [
    "Max IOB": "Maximum Insulin on Board (IOB)",
    "Max Bolus": "Maximum Bolus",
]
```

There are two cases where these are needed:
1. Abbreviated search terms - the search result/index uses a shorter/common name but the label within the UI shows the full name (e.g. "Max Basal" → "Maximum Basal Rate")
2. Multiple search terms targeting one section - several terms scroll to the same UI element
  - "Dark Mode", "Light Mode", and "Appearance" all scroll to the Appearance picker
  - "Cone" and "Lines" both scroll to the "Forecast Display Type"

2. Multiple search terms targeting one section - several terms scroll to the same UI element
  - "Dark Mode", "Light Mode", and "Appearance" all scroll to the Appearance picker
  - "Cone" and "Lines" both scroll to the "Forecast Display Type"

includedPullRequests.title: Shared BolusSafetyValidator for APNS and Shortcut boluses
includedPullRequests.body:
## Summary

Extracts a shared `BolusSafetyValidator` service that centralises the safety checks previously inlined in the APNS remote-bolus path, and applies them to Shortcut-driven boluses as well.

**Checks applied:** max bolus, max IOB, recent-bolus 20% threshold.

## Remote commands (APNS)

Behaviour is unchanged. `TrioRemoteControl+Bolus` now calls the validator with `lookbackStart: Date(timeIntervalSince1970: payload.timestamp)`, so the 20% recent-bolus check still covers the interval between the command being sent and now, and existing rejection messages are preserved. (Minor side-effect: an unavailable IOB now rejects with a logged message instead of throwing.)

## Shortcuts

Shortcut boluses previously checked only the "max bolus" limit. They now get the same full set of checks. Because the Shortcut has no caller-provided origin timestamp, the recent-bolus check uses a 6-minute rolling window (`BolusSafetyEvaluator.recentBolusWindowMinutes`).

Closes #531

includedPullRequests.title: Shared BolusSafetyValidator for APNS and Shortcut boluses
includedPullRequests.body:
## Summary

Extracts a shared `BolusSafetyValidator` service that centralises the safety checks previously inlined in the APNS remote-bolus path, and applies them to Shortcut-driven boluses as well.

**Checks applied:** max bolus, max IOB, recent-bolus 20% threshold.

## Remote commands (APNS)

Behaviour is unchanged. `TrioRemoteControl+Bolus` now calls the validator with `lookbackStart: Date(timeIntervalSince1970: payload.timestamp)`, so the 20% recent-bolus check still covers the interval between the command being sent and now, and existing rejection messages are preserved. (Minor side-effect: an unavailable IOB now rejects with a logged message instead of throwing.)

## Shortcuts

Shortcut boluses previously checked only the "max bolus" limit. They now get the same full set of checks. Because the Shortcut has no caller-provided origin timestamp, the recent-bolus check uses a 6-minute rolling window (`BolusSafetyEvaluator.recentBolusWindowMinutes`).

Closes #531

includedPullRequests.title: Fix Apple Health flag by checking for sample type permission
includedPullRequests.body:
A tester reported appleHealthEnabled: false despite a working Apple Health integration.

Cause: `hasGrantedFullWritePermissions` required all five Trio-managed sample types (glucose, insulin, carbs, fat, protein) to be authorized.

Changes in `TelemetryClient.buildPayload`: 
- `appleHealthEnabled` → true when any write permission is granted (matches the state-based nightscoutPaired /
  tidepoolPaired pattern already in place).
- New `appleHealthWrites: { glucose, insulin, carbs, fat, protein }`check per-type authorization snapshot. Reuses the existing `checkWriteToHealthPermissions` of HealthKitManager.

includedPullRequests.title: Fix Apple Health flag by checking for sample type permission
includedPullRequests.body:
A tester reported appleHealthEnabled: false despite a working Apple Health integration.

Cause: `hasGrantedFullWritePermissions` required all five Trio-managed sample types (glucose, insulin, carbs, fat, protein) to be authorized.

Changes in `TelemetryClient.buildPayload`: 
- `appleHealthEnabled` → true when any write permission is granted (matches the state-based nightscoutPaired /
  tidepoolPaired pattern already in place).
- New `appleHealthWrites: { glucose, insulin, carbs, fat, protein }`check per-type authorization snapshot. Reuses the existing `checkWriteToHealthPermissions` of HealthKitManager.
